functions.name: with_delimiter
functions.doc:
A dialect with an arbitrary single-character delimiter. A
delimiter that is not exactly one character, or that is the
quote character itself, is a programming error the parser
rejects with `BadDialect` rather than misbehaving silently.

functions.name: error_message
functions.doc:
A human-readable, backend-identical rendering of an error.
Positions are 1-based record numbers, the unit an operator
reasons about.

functions.name: parse
functions.doc: Parse CSV text with the default comma dialect.

functions.name: parse_with
functions.doc: Parse CSV text with an explicit dialect.

args: BadDialect("delimiter must be exactly one character, got '${...}'")
"delimiter must be exactly one character, got '${...}'"
BadDialect("delimiter cannot be the quote character")
BadDialect("delimiter cannot be a newline character")

functions.name: parse_headed
functions.doc:
Parse CSV text and take its first record as the header.
Empty input yields an empty header and no rows.

functions.name: parse_headed_with
functions.doc: Parse with an explicit dialect, first record as the header.

functions.name: from_table
functions.doc:
Build a header view from an already-parsed Table. The first
record becomes the columns; the rest become the data rows.

functions.name: length
functions.doc: The number of data records (header excluded).

functions.name: column_index
functions.doc: The zero-based index of a column, or None if absent.

functions.name: has_column
functions.doc: Whether the header declares a column by this name.

functions.name: get
functions.doc:
The value of `column` in data record `row_index`
(zero-based, header excluded). Errors with `NoSuchColumn`
when the header has no such column, with `NoSuchRow` when
there is no data record at `row_index`, and with `ShortRow`
when the record exists but has fewer fields than the
column's index.

Advisory { id: id.trim(), package: package.trim(), version: version.trim(), s...

operator_declared_grants.note: Authority declared by the operator at build/run time (e.g. --preopen). DISTINCT from the compiler-derived, program-proven capability surface; the compiler could not derive these grants.

compiler_derived_path_arg_surface.note: argv (env.args()) arguments the compiler PROVED reach an Fs / Net / Env sink, and whether read or write. A sound over-approximation (no reaching argument omitted; arg_index '*' when the concrete index is not statically determinate). Closures are covered SOUND BY CONSTRUCTION: a closure whose parameter reaches a sink is reported at '*' UNLESS the compiler proves it is applied only locally to non-argv values -- so a closure that escapes its frame (returned, passed to a helper, stored in an aggregate, or reached through a match/if arm or a name bound to one) fails closed, with no per-shape enumeration. Scope is EXHAUSTIVELY covered: the statement traversal descends into EVERY sub-scope that holds sub-statements -- if/while/for statement blocks, match-arm blocks, and lambda bodies, at any nesting depth -- so a binding or sink in any sub-block (including a match-arm block) is never skipped (pinned by a meta-test over the AST node inventory). RESIDUAL (the one honest gap, VALUE-FLOW only): a closure carried by a value the AST-level pass cannot statically tie back to a lambda -- re-extracted from a runtime container by key, or threaded through an opaque computed value -- may be under-reported. This does NOT grant authority -- a dynamic Fs/Net path still fail-closes in --wasi without --preopen; it is an auditable fact, distinct from operator_declared_grants.
